title: Wyethia subresinosa
taxonomy: Wyethia subresinosa belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Wyethia, which consists of small, daisy-like plants native to North America. The plant is closely related to other members of the Asteraceae family, such as the sunflowers and daisies. Wyethia subresinosa is further classified as a shrub or perennial herb, depending on the specific growth habits of the plant. The classification of Wyethia subresinosa is also supported by its characteristic flowers, which resemble those of other Asteraceae family members.

intro: Wyethia subresinosa, commonly known as Subresinous Mule's Ears or Western Mule's Ears, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Wyethia subresinosa contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited to traditional and folk medicine due to a lack of modern scientific research.
